extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR won on Super Planche des Belles Filles, catching breakaway Kämna inside 100m and distancing all GC rivals except Vingegaard by 12+ seconds.
tactical_patterns: UAE deployed three-man pace train (McNulty-Bennett-Majka) before gravel section, preventing attacks until steepest gradient where domestiques exhausted.
Pogacar recalled teammate from breakaway mid-stage, prioritizing team control over stage hunting when yellow jersey margin only 4 seconds.
Vingegaard launched winning move at 100m remaining, too late on 24% gravel gradient where acceleration superiority matters less than position.
Geschke suffered bridging Kämna under 5km despite being in break, revealing cost of attacking riders already in breakaway on final climb.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: isolated second place 12 seconds down suggests matching Pogacar's power but lacks decisive acceleration on extreme gradients
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: finished third at 12 seconds, mirroring Vingegaard's gap but showed no attack intent on climb that dropped him in 2020 TT
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: distanced on gravel section alongside Yates despite sitting in, suggesting weakness on explosive 24% pitches rather than sustained climbing
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: held solo lead until final 100m after bridging Geschke sub-5km, extraordinary diesel power betrayed only by gravel gradient
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: dropped from GC group before final 5km while teammate Kämna soloed, Bora depth concerns for mountain support
rider_signals.GESCHKE Simon: negative: KOM jersey holder attacked but cracked when Kämna bridged, cannot sustain both breakaway effort and late-climb accelerations
